intro: Product photography is where AI moved fastest from novelty to infrastructure: the platforms sellers already use, Amazon, Google, Shopify, now generate the imagery themselves. The pattern to teach is which work automates first, and which stays a human job.

trends.title: AI staging tools sell themselves as studio replacements
trends.body: Photoroom and Flair.ai openly market AI-generated product scenes as a substitute for a studio shoot, with industry guides claiming 80 to 95 percent cost reduction per image against $200 to $5,000 traditional sessions.

trends.title: Marketplaces build generation directly into the seller tools
trends.body: Amazon's AI Creative Studio turns a basic product photo into lifestyle imagery and video inside Seller Central; Amazon Ads reported roughly 5 percent more sales for advertisers using its Image Generator. Google's Product Studio does free text-to-image product staging in Merchant Center and via Shopify.

trends.title: Catalog-scale consistency, not one-off hero images, is the real product
trends.body: Photoroom's pitch is standardizing lighting and background across large SKU catalogs; Flair leans toward conceptual lifestyle scenes. The useful distinction for students is production tool versus creative tool.

trends.title: In-context editing makes compositing prompt-based
trends.body: Flux.1 Kontext landed inside Adobe Photoshop's beta Generative Fill in September 2025 as a selectable model, and Google's Nano Banana Pro now powers image generation inside Google Ads directly.

tools.name: Photoroom
tools.note: AI product photo backgrounds and catalog-scale batch editing

tools.name: Amazon AI Creative Studio
tools.note: Built into Seller Central, photo to lifestyle image/video

tools.name: Google Product Studio
tools.note: Free text-to-image product staging in Merchant Center / Shopify

tools.name: Adobe Firefly / Photoshop Generative Fill
tools.note: Now includes a Flux Kontext model option for in-context edits

campaigns.title: The clearest citable performance number in this category
campaigns.body: No single DTC brand campaign in product photography surfaced with independently verifiable results. The strongest citable case is Amazon's own reported figure: advertisers using its AI Image Generator saw roughly 5 percent more sales.

controversies.title: Product truthfulness
controversies.body: AI-generated scenes risk misrepresenting a physical product's real scale, texture, or color, a live version of the same false-advertising concern found in the food category's AI menu photos.

controversies.title: Commoditized packshot work is the most automated segment
controversies.body: General industry commentary consistently frames the shift as: photographers who only shot flat packshots are repositioning as CGI or AI art directors, while concept-driven hero photography stays a human specialty.
